Chafaye was always timely in responding to all of my emails.my emails. She clearly explained answers to my questions. I would definitely recommend Montana Estate Lawyers in Great Falls, Montana.

We are grateful for the estate planning that Jon McCarty and team provided our family.  Extremely thorough planning including the ability to customize.  Very transparent and fair regarding fees and costs.  Jon has also been easy to work with regarding updates to the plan as our needs have changed.  Highly recommended.

When we first called Montana Estate Lawyers, there were so many things going on that were all new experiences for us.
We were in dire need of an estate lawyer and we called several. We landed upon Montana Estate Lawyers from their reviews online and because they ACTUALLY returned our call when they said that they would!!
We had a call with Trevor Funseth and unfurled our laundry list of questions and concerns. On that very first call,
he made us feel that we NO LONGER HAD TO WORRY.
We were finally in good hands!
He patiently answered each question and made sure

We were always taken care of with Trevor's returned calls, punctual and precise advice and a kind and knowledgeable voice.
We have appreciated Trevor and Montana Estate Lawyers for how they helped us get through a very challenging time.

We rarely write reviews but we had to after this experience!
We HIGHLY recommend this team to help with any Estate issues-
Thank you for taking such good care of us!!
👍
I have been using them for more than 10 years, great service

Jon has done an amazing job for me. After 2 special needs trust funds I finally ended up at his office…. I now can say with confidence that my son will be ok. Jon made it so my son’s physician, medications, etc are all easily found,  I might have overlooked much of it. He also has gone the extra mile when tragedy  struck my family and I needed to change my will.  Within the week he had everything changed. I know he honestly cares about my son and I would highly recommend them to anyone.

We were introduced to Keith Tokerud of Montana Estate Lawyers by our financial advisor in 2010, when we asked him if he could recommend someone who could help us update our estate plans after we moved to Montana.  Keith reviewed what we previously had in place and did an excellent job of explaining available options to help us meet our estate planning needs and goals.  After our initial plans were in place, we have used their "Peace of Mind Protector Program" to keep our estate planning current as our situation has changed over time and as laws and regulations have changed.  Keith and his staff have all been super to work with, both personally and professionally.  We highly recommend Montana Estate Lawyers if you are in need of estate planning services.
All staff are personable, and professional.

Jon McCarty was recommended to us by a lawyer friend when we were searching for a firm to help us with estate planning. In the ten years Jon has worked with us, we have found him to be highly professional, extremely knowledgeable and compassionate. You can trust Jon to help you plan this very important aspect of your life.

I have worked with Scott, Tokerud & McCarty, P.C. since the autumn of 2018.  They have been very helpful in guiding me through the process of obtaining the Guardianship and Conservatorship of my mother, who is sadly incapacitated due to dementia.

When I found myself in need for an Elder Law Attorney, I was living a time zone away from Great Falls.  I was not able to travel to Great Falls from my home in Northern Minnesota, where I had relocated for a job.  I was fortunate to have had Jon McCarty as a neighbor when I lived in Great Falls, so I had gotten to know him really well when I was living next to him.  In getting to know Jon, I found him to be a compassionate, conscientious, intelligent, and honorable man.  I could think of nobody else in Montana to whom I could turn for advice and guidance in navigating the process for Guardianship and Conservatorship of my incapacitated mother.  If Jon had not been an Elder Law Attorney, I still would have turned to him for advice to determine the best person to help me in my situation.

When my siblings and I realized that my mother required a Guardian and Conservator after my father passed away, we had no idea what it would take to accomplish the undertaking.  None of us lived in the mountain time zone anymore and none of us knew the first thing about what was entailed in the process.  Jon McCarty and the staff at Scott, Tokerud & McCarty, P.C. (LaTonya and Chris) have always been there to explain the legal requirements, the estate information, and the evaluations that are necessary to become and remain a Guardian and Conservator.  They set up appointments with the nurse evaluator and my mother’s legal counselor in the initial part of the process to achieving Guardianship and Conservatorship.  They filed the paperwork, attended all legal hearings, and represented us well.  Every year, they send me a reminder and guidance on the annual reports that I need to make to the court in regard to my management of my mother’s affairs as Guardian and Conservator and they file the necessary paperwork as well as attend requisite legal hearings in those instances, too.  I consider them to be part of my mother’s care team, just as much as the nurses and administrators in the memory care center where she lives.  What is even more astounding is that I can tell that they all care deeply about my mother, even though they have never met her!

If you are considering hiring Jon McCarty for Elder Law or any aspect of Estate Planning, I would highly encourage you to do so.  Jon McCarty and the good people at Scott, Tokerud & McCarty, P.C. truly exemplify Montana values.  They believe in hard work (and they do work hard), doing the right thing, helping a neighbor out, and celebrating the blessings and good things that happen in life.  They believe in accountability and reliability, and they will definitely be on your side and treat you like you are one of their very special family.  They have been there for me and my family in our time of need, and I cannot imagine how we could have managed without them there to guide and support us.  I feel certain that they will offer you the same wisdom, comfort, and support as well.

Went to them to do our wills.  Explained what we wanted.  Wills were prepared and unfortunately, executed to early.  Went back in to the office to see Jon.  He was very compassionate.  Thank you for taking care us.  I recommend your office to everyone I talk to.  Great people to do business with.
Working with Jon McCarty through the on-line system has been a valuable experience.  I highly recommend his services whether in-person or online.
I can’t say enough about John and Mary. They went above and beyond for my family. Always quick to respond, courteous and provide updates. Highly recommend this firm.

I was involved in a 5 vehicle accident, including a semi that was at fault, I just wanted it over and couldn't even get the other insurance company to return my calls after a month. I went to Towe & Fitzpatrick and met with John and Mary, they took it from there and dealt with everything for me; all the medical bills, the adjusters, the trucking company, the other ins. company and their lawyer. I couldn't have asked for a better group of people to work with and for me. They got me a fair settlement that without them I'm sure would have been nothing. Thank u Towe and Fitzpatrick for everything. Sincerely Michael j. Grenfell

Beck, Amsden & Stalpes represented my family after the death of my husband through a very challenging Workers Comp case. Anthony mentored myself and my  daughters through the very painful process and made it an opportunity for us to find closure and strength by being able to advocate for their Dad.  John was exceptional in overseeing the difficult process and always providing reassurance and clear answers to my questions.  Mike and Monte both were involved with the trial itself and played key roles in the success.  It was an all around team effort.  The entire staff at Beck, Amsden & Stalpes was always available, answered questions and provided reassurance throughout.  I highly recommend this firm for all of your legal needs!

I would highly recommend this firm over and over again. I specifically worked with Anthony and he was absolutely amazing to work with. I had no idea what I was doing or needed to do in my situation and he was extremely helpful in explaining what my role looked like and what exactly he needed from me in order to complete his job. I will forever be grateful for the hard work and dedication that was put into my case as well as the above and beyond efforts that Anthony did to make sure that I was treated fairly by all parties involved. If I am ever in a situation again where I need legal help, Beck, Amsden & Stalpes will be the first place I call. I can't thank them enough.

I can’t thank them enough and the work that they’ve done for me has inspired me to get into pre-law and eventually law school so I can make positive differences in people’s lives like they did for me. This is an amazing, respectable, trustworthy, and amiable firm. I highly recommend anyone and everyone to and trust these attorneys at law.
Matt has gone above and beyond for all my legal needs. He is very knowledgeable in all aspects and is quick to respond and solve any issues that arise along the way!

In 2014 I was diagnosed with Lewy Body Dementia and by 2015 I was forced to retire from my position with the State of Montana. In 2016, I applied for SSDI, which was denied and it took 2 1/2 years to win an appeal, along with an apology from the judge for it even going to appeal. In 2018 I was given retroactive SSDI for  2016-2018, which in turn was required used to reimburse my private disability insurance that I was living on during that time. Unfortunately, as a result I had to refile those 3 years replacing taxable SSDI for the nontaxable insurance, resulting in owing back taxes over those 3 years. I secured the Morrison Law Firm to help me and they negotiated a settlement with the IRS and Montana Dept. of Revenue. The firm helping me to financially fight my neurological diseases with costly medical care without a tax debt hanging over my head. Tom Morrison and his staff were remarkably compassionate and committed to my case. Research Prosecutorial misconduct as prosecutorial misconduct can only happen when lawyers, both sides defense and prosecution fail to follow their code of ethics. I lost my job, my children lost 4 years of their development in their sports, education, and life. They allowed the prosecutors to alter my house for a jury view (the sports vase given to me by my son's football team for coaching), knew of lies being told in court, fail to communicate to me a very important argument in my case about a dresser and something that never happened in court was able to be altered after trial.
In Mooney v. Holohan, 294 U.S. 103 (1935), prosecutors “knowingly” used perjured testimony.

Attached you will see that in the hand written statement, she wrote that I left the room. Then in trial her story changed to where she left the room. The prosecutors knew of this lie as her story changed, and they continued it. To confuse the jury more, this fabricated lie, I was never interviewed by the detective or asked about it on tape. So there was no audio of any discussion I had about this lie with the detective, with me answering to the lie. Then the prosecutors road this lie in court, fabricating the lie as well. There is a dresser in my boy’s room where she claimed this to have happened that have always been the way it is blocking the opening she claimed to have gotten away from me in trial. I was never made aware of the importance of that argument by my lawyers; when the judge asked about it in chamber, I was confused as I did not know anything about the dresser argument; as it states in the lawyer's professional code of ethic Rule 1.4 Communication (a) A Lawyer shall: 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5
Yet we are supposed to trust lawyers as our human rights is about our liberty. America's system of law is nothing but manipulations and fabrication. As it stated in a CIA book, my people can never trust America,. that was at a macro scale. what happened to me is at an individual scale but with the same affect.
Vayeeleng

reviews.text: John is not only a great lawyer, but also a great guy. Him and his staff does a very good job.

Awesome Lawyer

I worked with Caleb  and found him to be extremely diligent, hardworking, and knowledgeable.  I never had to wait more than 24 hours for a response unless he was out of town. I found his billing to be highly ethical and he never overcharged me on hours. Top notch-you just can't do any better

If you want an attorney who attentively calls you back, hire Caleb Simpson. Not only prompt, he displays the ‘customer service’ that most attorneys lack- he takes the time to explain the process and educate the client realistically on what you can and cannot expect. His communication skills are impressive, listening closely to the information you deliver and then synthesizing fragments to come up with a comprehensive understanding of your case. You will be impressed with his professional, confident demeanor, expertise in multiple facets of law and dedication to helping you.
Pat G gets shit done
Very helpful.
Got some good advice

Pat had a very good perspective on my case. Attentive to my details and he offered great advice. I have a better frame of mind and grasp of what is in front of me because of a great consultation. Thank you

Exceptional experience having Lucy and Melissa on my case.  Very experienced, knowledgeable and professional. The communication and preparation was outstanding, prepared for everything.  Prompt communication and updates through the entire process. Lucy came highly recommended, not only from attorneys, not even in family law, but from a former high level Judge of the judicial branch who had presided over her cases. They took my case on short notice, Lucy was the first one I called and I knew very quickly why.  We never met in person till the day of, as we are on opposite sides of the state, which didn't phase them one bit.  Very easy to work with, even remotely. Her court room presence was impressive to say the least.

As a client, let me say this clearly: Lucy Hansen is not just an attorney. She is the voice of reason when you’re overwhelmed, and the fighter you didn’t even know you needed. When we hired Lucy, our situation was complicated, emotional, and honestly… terrifying. From day one, she made us feel seen, heard, and supported. She never treated our case like paperwork; she treated it like people. She fought for our child with a level of passion and care I didn’t know attorneys were capable of.

She told us the truth even when it was hard. She protected our child’s best interests every step of the way. And she carried some of that emotional weight right alongside us, which says everything about who she is as a human, not just a lawyer.

Lucy doesn’t operate from ego or money. She operates from purpose. She genuinely wants good, honest outcomes for the families she represents, and that is painfully rare in this field.
She truly makes you feel like you’re not facing any of this alone.

If you want an attorney who will show up, stand up, and stay steady for your family, do not hesitate.
Lucy changed the trajectory of our case, and our child’s life.
Hiring her was the best decision we made.

Lucy is a wonderful attorney.  She is professional, knowledgeable, and organized. She is responsive and always made me feel like I was in the best hands. I could not be happier with her work, and she is a lovely person as a bonus!

Hands down, the absolute best lawyer.
Lucy’s dedication and genuine care made all the difference in achieving the best possible outcome for us. Her hard work has been a constant source of confidence, no matter what challenges came our way. I would wholeheartedly recommend Lucy to anyone in need of a skilled and compassionate lawyer

I can’t say enough good things about working with Lucy. From the very first consultation, she was attentive, knowledgeable, and completely dedicated to my case. She took the time to explain every step in a way that made me feel confident and informed throughout the entire process. Her professionalism, responsiveness, and ability to navigate complex issues truly set her apart. Lucy went above and beyond to secure the best possible outcome, and I always felt like she had my best interest at heart. If you’re looking for an attorney who is not only highly skilled but also genuinely cares, Lucy is the one you want on your side. Highly recommended! - Thomas

Lucy was incredible. She was honest, caring, and very communicative. I am a worrier and like to be informed every step of the way and Lucy was very understanding of my needs and was more than willing to help me feel content and in the loop.

Lucy and her team were amazing. She took the time to listen and understand my unique situation. She was efficient and caring. Needing an attorney is never fun, but Lucy made it the best it could possibly be. I couldn't have done it without her and her team!

Scott Pederson is an exceptional human being who represented me in a dissolution of marriage. He advocated on my behalf in the utmost professional fashion. The man is truly a sincere person, and his passion for helping people in difficult situations is so evident. If you are so lucky as to have Scott on your side, you are lucky enough.

Lucy is an amazing advocate for her clients. I'm so thankful for her. Absolutely don't hesitate to seek her representation. She's incredibly passionate and committed to serving her clients to the best of her abilities and getting the best possible outcomes.

Thank you Lucy!

Lucy represented me in an order of protection proceeding. She was a fierce advocate for me. Lucy is smart, driven, organized and compassionate. She is who I refer anyone to when asked for a family law attorney.

Lucy has done an outstanding  job for me. She has been my number 1 for the better  part of a decade. I love her tendency and her professionalism.  I highly  recommend her and have to many people.  She's amazing at what she does.

I’ve worked in the Missoula family law arena for five years collectively. When my sister needed an attorney, I without hesitation sent her to Lucy. She won at trial and on appeal. Lucy is an attorney that will truly fight for you.

If you're looking for a family law attorney, Lucy Hansen is it. She truly cares about her clients, and especially cares about the safety and wellbeing of children. That was something I did not find in the previous attorney I had worked with, nor is it what I saw in the opposing counsel. She helped me achieve what I was told was nearly impossible. It's truly unusual to find a lawyer that that not only has a brain but has a heart. And I cannot emphasize enough how important that is when you're dealing with family court.

I highly recommend Lucy. She has been my attorney for several years and has done an outstanding job. Lucy is very knowledgeable and dedicated to her clients. Lucy and her staff have always been professional and worked very hard helping our family through difficult times.

For approximately 1 year I was involved in a Montana State Common-Law Marriage Dissolution.
My research for an attorney that would fit my particular needs was troublesome and exhausting at times to say the least, and terminating one attorney.
My search came to an end when I retained Lucy from Hansen Law Firm located in Missoula, MT.
Lucy was very experienced in these type of claims and throughout my ordeal she was diligent, informative and compassionate. I knew there was a long road ahead for me but with these qualities and much more Lucy was able to secure an ending to this experience that I was happy with, and to add "very reasonable"!
I highly recommend Lucy for any of your legal needs.
Darby, MT.

Since 2015 we have had
Lucy Hansen represent our needs.  You will NOT find a more knowledgeable, determined, absolutely dedicated to your cause and representation professional.
So very thankful for  Lucy’s background, her intuitive perspective, dedication and compassion for our position.  Highly recommend Lucy Hansen, she’s a professional, a class act Lady with the fight of a warrior and a heart of a parent. Thank You Lucy  you are held in very high regard.

I have worked with Lucy Hansen for several years through my divorce proceeding, settlement conference and subsequent child custody proceedings. I was lucky to have her on my side, guiding me through one of the most difficult times of my life.

I reached out to Lonabaugh & Riggs while seeking advice on setting up a trust and navigating my estate planning needs. From the very first call, I was impressed. The attorney was knowledgeable, patient, and transparent about what I needed to do, as well as the services they could provide. There was no pressure to commit to anything; it was simply sound, professional advice tailored to my circumstances.

It’s rare to find a law firm that prioritizes client education and trust-building from the outset. Lonabaugh & Riggs exceeded my expectations, and I now feel confident moving forward with my estate planning. If you are looking for experienced, straightforward legal advice, I highly recommend giving them a call.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Novae,

We can’t seem to find any record of you ever having been a client of our office.

We are a small firm. Unfortunately, there are times when our existing caseload makes it impossible for us to accept new clients. This is in no way a reflection on a potential client or the viability of his or her case; it is simply a means of ensuring that we are able to provide our existing clients with the best possible service.

I hope this helps to clear up any confusion as to why we may have been unable to take on a potential case. Best of luck in the future.

reviews.text: A top notch law firm that’s been a part of downtown Missoula for over 40 years.

If I could give no stars, I would. Very unprofessional with all communication with their clientele. Was told by defense attorney there would be no jail time associated with the charges, but I still received a week of jail time due to advised plea agreement. After release from jail time, received letter from law firm notifying me of an additional $1200 bill after paying initial $5,000 dollars which was told to be estimated cost of all legal fees. Firm did not even give proper allotment of time for payment of additional $1200 when I called to schedule payment arrangements for money not agreed upon initially. I really do try my best to see the best in everyone, but this firm will never receive any of my friends, family, or even acquaintances business. Worst law firm in the Valley, 10/10 DO NOT RECOMMEND.

reviews.text: Ineffective counsel - provided solution that least benefited me and made me as responsible for the negligence of my co-trustee when I wasn’t to blame.  I was given only one solutions that tied me to their on-going attorney fees when MT allows Trust supervision to be given back to the trustees - but, I wasn’t told that.  I was charged for work I specifically declined to move forward with but she did anyways.  She provided a plan, describing it as “my fiduciary responsibility,”began the court papers to later provide a clause in my Trust to change her mind. To top it off, I wasn’t given my full retainer back AND wasn’t given it at all  until a month after signing their withdrawal docs.  Do not sign with this firm.

No ethics at all.

reviews.text: I have worked with Tiffany before on a family matter and she was not only professional, prepared, and knowledgeable, but she was incredibly compassionate.  I liked Tiffany's approach to my case, because she was compassionate but she was also very realistic in explaining how family law is difficult because no case is the same as another, and the law is not black and white.  I was going through an awful parenting case that involved abuse and she worked hard to get the best possible deal for me and my children.  We were able to settle my case at mediation and she presented my case better than I ever could have imagined.  My children and me were in excellent hands.  If you are looking for an attorney who knows the law and is incredibly supportive, I highly recommend Tiffany for your family law needs.  She also helped me form a business as I was rebuilding after my divorce and she was incredibly knowledgeable in this area of the law too.  I intend on returning to her for my estate planning needs. reviews.text:
I had the unfortunate experience of watching Tiffany Nunnally litigate the divorce of my parents, giving me an interesting perspective, as I have an unbiased opinion of both parties and want what’s best for both of them-equally. It is my opinion that Ms. Nunnally exploited certain situations surrounding the divorce. The kindness and understanding she portrayed to the unrepresented parent followed by sneaky phone calls to real estate agents and billing for minuscule amounts of work were demoralizing to the firm she works for and manipulative. She appeared to be more concerned with getting paid than dealing with the actual legal work. If you are seeking an overtly aggressive attorney and money is not of concern, I recommend Ms. Nunnally. If you are interested in a experienced attorney who isn’t manipulative, attempts to settle your divorce proceedings with dignity, while having awareness of what their work is actually worth, I suggest you look elsewhere. Furthermore, with regard to a non-emergent legal matter, only a morally corrupt lawyer would serve legal paperwork to someone on Christmas Eve. Also, it’s odd that Tiffany’s family (Nate Nunnally) is writing a review for her on here and suggests that they cannot find genuine reviews based off their work alone.

** In response to the response of this review, will you be charging the client to discuss my review? Please advise. Thanks.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Hebert - We are sorry to hear that you are not satisfied with the Tiffany's work for your family member. Unfortunately, being on the outside of the matter you were likely not privy to all of the on goings in the matter. Since our business is in the field of law we cannot discuss the ins and outs of the matter with you. However, we will reach out directly to our client to discuss your concerns.

We hope you are staying healthy and safe. - Cassie Dellwo, Five Valleys Law, P.L.L.C.

She didn’t just listen—she heard me. She immediately made me feel more at ease, calmly walking me through the process and answering every question I had with honesty, patience, and clarity.

Throughout the entire experience, Natalie was everything I could have hoped for in an attorney: incredibly competent, unwaveringly compassionate, and remarkably communicative. She made what could have been a nightmare feel manageable and kept me informed every step of the way. I never felt lost or in the dark because she was always just a phone call or email away, ready with answers and support.

Natalie Walz is an absolute gem—intelligent, grounded, and deeply human in her approach to law. If you’re looking for an attorney who will not only represent you with skill but treat you with genuine care and respect, look no further. With a former prosecutor on their team, they have a unique ability to anticipate the prosecution’s strategies, allowing them to craft highly effective defenses. Their extensive experience and strong courtroom presence consistently lead to outstanding results.

Watson Law's dedication to their clients is unwavering and evident in every case they take on. If you’re seeking a defense firm that blends sharp legal expertise with a personalized touch, Watson Law is the best option in Bozeman, making you feel valued and important.

When I was first Looking for a Lawyer with particular expertise,  every professional I talked to wanted so much money to be compensated for a "consultation".  After running through so many of these professionals who are already in a profession that charges obscene amounts of "billing",  in a glutted market, where their ads claim ""compassion" and "client service",  I happened to call a Lawyer who actually ANSWERED THE PHONE in his own personal voice.  This person was Nick LeTang,  and over the course of almost an hour,  he gave me all the essential information I needed.  He not only did this,  but did it without regard to "looking at the clock",  but trying to answer my questions with the degree of "compassion"  and attention to "client services"  that all these other firms purported to represent.  He did this FREE OF CHARGE.  He not only knew what I wanted,  but anticipated what I would require.  When I went to see him for an appointment,  he again went through all my questions,  answered them thoroughly,  was totally respectful,  and instead of giving me some open ended figure as to fees,  gave me a flat rate structure.  Again,  he did not charge for the appointment.  This is not something I would expect on a regular basis,  but to find a Person of such integrity and understanding to every individual situation,  and to possess such a regard for a  client's well- being rather than how much they get for their time,  is something very rare in this world.  It is the reason why I do recommend Nick LeTang so highly,  but in the same perspective,  do NOT want him to get TOO busy,  because I'd like to keep him all for myself.  He is that much of a Great professional,  and through his dedication and putting his customer's needs first,  is a very rare and wonderful person.  Thank you,  Nick,  for being a bright beacon in your profession,  and  being a Kind and Caring  example of what the Legal profession should be.

reviews.text:
Outstanding Legal Representation and Genuine Advocacy
We are beyond grateful for the exceptional legal counsel provided by Marcel Quinn and Seth Rogers in our civil litigation matter. Faced with a wrongful lawsuit, we felt overwhelmed and frustrated—but from our first meeting, Marcel and Seth demonstrated an extraordinary level of diligence, efficiency, and legal acumen that immediately set us at ease.  They were thorough in examining the complexities of our case yet consistently made the legal process understandable and manageable. Their ability to distill intricate legal issues into clear, actionable steps was invaluable. As quick studies with sharp legal insight, they immediately grasped the nuances of our situation and applied their expertise toward achieving exactly the resolution we had hoped for.  What stood out even more was their consistent communication. At every turn, they kept us informed, empowered, and reassured. Not only did they validate our concerns and treat our situation with compassion, but they made it their mission to pursue a just and successful outcome with unwavering professionalism and efficiency.  Thanks to their efforts, we were able to move forward with confidence and clarity. Marcel Quinn and Seth Rogers are everything you hope for in legal representation—responsive, perceptive, and deeply invested in their clients. We offer our sincere thanks and highest recommendation.

Marcel Quinn and her team were absolutely fantastic! I'm not law savy and she broke things down so that I was comfortable and understood. Communication was important for me and they were always one step ahead. I was very pleased with my experience with this law firm.

Hammer, Quinn & Shaw are phenomenal.  I have been utilizing their expertise on both a professional and a personal level for over 7 years.  In all of those interactions, I have been presented with excellent legal advice.  They have always maintained the highest level of integrity and never shy away from presenting the best course of action for the company, the employee or in personal matters.
Our business has used Hammer, Quinn & Shaw for multiple insurance cases. Our attorney Marcel Quinn was knowledgeable about our cases and communicated with us throughout the process.
Ben Hammer was up front and kept me informed throughout the whole process, very pleased will use Ben again if need be, thanks for the good work

Our attorney, Tom Hollo, was very truthful and realistic with regards to our case about my brothers Estate. He did an excellent job in preparing, preping us before court and while in the Court knew what he was doing and how to do it. Without any paper evidence the Judge was convinced that my version of the events was the more correct version and awarded all that my brother had wanted to leave us to my siblings and I. He was very professional throughout along with being personable. We are very glad that we ended up in his capable hands.

I have worked with Marcel Quinn and her firm on several matters and have always found them to be extremely courteous, knowledgeable, efficient, effective and professional.  Keeping them on a short list of "highly recommended" attorneys.
We love working with Todd and Marcel.  The entire team has been helpful, knowledgeable and timely with any matter we have had!

Two other law firms were  unable to make progress on an easement issue with my property , but Hammer, Quinn and Shaw provided excellent service and resolved the issue quickly. If you need professional representation, use them.

My wife and I came to see Tyler Mullowney at his office on April 25, 2019, and told him that our neighbor had destroyed our driveway.
I told him of repairs I have had to make to keep our driveway open and usable. I told him that we've had zero cooperation from her; it was time to take her to court for payment of repairs to our driveway, and most importantly, to enforce existing covenants. I carefully explained that I needed to hire an attorney to handle the LITIGATION. He responded by saying, "I think I can help you." It seems that was a lie. 7 months and $627 later (Nov. 19, 2019) we again came to see Mr. Mullowney. He said, "I don't do litigation." The next month he told me that a local retired attorney had agreed to litigate the driveway case and that he, Tyler, would facilitate the litigation. (Another lie but it got another $189 out of me). I hope to never in my life meet another person like Tyler Mullowney.

Update: All claims and sanction requests from Lee Law office against me were dismissed with Prejudice by the Court. The case resolved to basically what was in the original contract before Lee Law office got involved and removed from the case (minus the approximate $100,000+ overall additional legal fees that accrued from the other side in my opinion from using Lee Law office). Use your own judgement on how you would want a law office responding and behaving. I would use one a bit more professional.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Broughton was a self-represented plaintiff in a civil suit filed against his uncle, who our firm represents.  He is not and has never been a client of our firm. Mr. Broughton's claims about our firm and the attorneys that work here are not true, with the exception of his claim that we prevented our client from speaking with Mr. Broughton about the case without our client's legal counsel present during such discussions.  As a result of Mr. Broughton's conduct in the pending civil case including making slanderous claims against our firm and its attorneys, our firm sought sanctions against Mr. Broughton.  The Court's ruling on this motion for sanctions has been temporarily stayed by the Court but it will, in our opinion, eventually be granted.  A review of the other Google reviews Mr. Broughton has left for other business or individuals shows he has a habit of leaving very negative reviews for people or businesses he doesn't like. This comports with his scorched earth approach utilized in the pending litigation before he was prohibited by the Court from filing any documents that were not first reviewed by a licensed Montana attorney.  He has also since been removed as the trustee of the trust which he was acting as in the pending litigation.

reviews.text:
If you are looking for an attorney who truly understands animal advocacy—especially for bully breeds—look no further than Bill Rideg.

Bill stepped in to help save our pit bulls, Raya and Hank, at a time when they were facing the kind of unfair scrutiny that pitties too often endure. He was thoughtful, strategic, deeply knowledgeable, and relentless in the best way. He understood not just the law, but the realities of breed bias and the importance of strong, principled advocacy for dogs who cannot speak for themselves.

What stood out most was Bill’s integrity and compassion. He put in extensive time and effort because he believed it was the right thing to do—not because it was easy or lucrative. Thanks to his work, our dogs are safe.

If you need an attorney who will fight for animals with intelligence, heart, and courage, Bill Rideg is exactly that advocate.
Woof - Montana Pittie Project
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Renee & everyone at Montana Pittie Project. Hoping for a long and healthy life for sweet Raya and Hank.

reviews.text:
I would have given 10 stars if I could!

I was being investigated for a crime that I did not commit and I called several law firms in town before speaking with Abby. The responses(from the receptionist) from the other firms I called was generally "okay, well I'll let ___ attorney know you called and they may get back to you in a week or so" . I understand that people are busy and can't always just jump on a call and having hired multiple lawyers previously (divorce), I was aware that the initial consult(s) can take a while.

I did a small amount of research, and Abby's resume was impressive so I put her on my short list of attorney's to call. When I called her firm, Ryan Miller Law, the receptionist picked up and I thought  "okay, I let her know you called and she'll get back to you". Instead, the receptionist checked with Abby and she immediately took my call and spoke to me.

Again, as mentioned, I've hired several attorney's previous to Abby, so I've gone through the consult process, and they're generally (in my experience) not overly reassuring, but when I spoke with Abby, I could feel my cortisol levels begin to drop. After hearing what was going on, she was confident that she could help, and she did a good job at explaining the process and potential outcomes in layman's terms that I could understand.

I decided to hire her, but doing so was going to be financially difficult as I was already paying two other attorney's (divorce in two states), Abby had sympathy for this and worked with me to set up installments for payment.

Leading up to my interview with the detective, Abby and her wonderful Paralegal, Maya, put everything together with impeccable attention to detail and organization. Abby brought me in to the office to prep for the interview, her and Maya also did an amazing job at not only preparing me, but also helping me feel confident in a very stressful time; the stress relief alone was worth the money!

My interview with the detective went well all due to Abby and Maya's work.

After the interview, Abby continued to provide me with updates and checked in on my general well-being.

Abby will be a force to be reckoned with for a long time, and I couldn't think of anyone else I would want on my side!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Caleb, thank you so much for this amazing and thoughtful review. Those words don't just mean so much to us, but they truly motivate us to continue to do what we do. We are so happy we could help you through this process. We are always here for you if you ever need anything else!
- RMC team

reviews.text:
AVOID AT ALL COSTS: Rasmussen Refused Consultation, Disrespectful, and Lazy

I was involved in an incident where a women ran me over intentionally, in a parking lot. I sustained a broken hand and a back injury. I was referred to this firm by LegalShield and was ready to hire Mr. Rasmussen, but his shocking lack of professionalism prevented me from ever even getting a consultation. I spent significant time providing his assistant with detailes, such as:  multiple witnesses, medical docmentation, police incident report and the existance of a surveillance video.
After reviewing my comprehensive file, Mr. Rasmussen didn't bother calling. Instead, I received a cold email stating he wouldn't meet because I only had an "incident report," not a "police report." When I replied, explaining the basic necessity of the need to file a court summons to obtain the surveillance evidence, because it was on private property (an aspect of basic law that he should have already been aware of) along with the fact that a surveillance video wouldn't even be needed in my case since I have multiple witnesses and medical documentation. Even with all these details Mr Rasmussen's final, dismissive response to me via email was simply: "Please start the process yourself."

He didn't have the decency or professionalism to pick up the phone for a 30-minute consultation to discuss the case. This attorney treats potential clients like objects that can be tossed in the trash. Instead of me getting the representation I was hoping for, so that I can focus on getting healed up from this terrible incident. He wants me to worry about submitting court documents that you hire an attorney for? This guy is a joke. I was able to call another attorney who took my call right away, and made me feel like my life and my injuries mattered. This attorney was also disgusted with the way I was treated by Mr Rasmussen over a surveillance video that would most likely never be needed.
Save your energy and your money. Do not waste your time trying to get an appointment with Mr. Rasmussen.
reviews.ownerResponse: Unfortunately, I cannot take every case and there are times when I just do not have 30 minutes because of the demands of my case load. I am glad you found an attorney to help you. Best of luck!

Eric and his staff are extremely responsive and kept me abreast of everything regarding my case. I will most definitely seek Eric's legal assistance in the future should a situation arise. If I could give Brandon 10 stars, I would. From the moment I hired him, I knew I had made the right decision. Brandon is, without question, the most experienced trial attorney in the state of Wyoming. His courtroom presence, strategic intellect, and deep knowledge of the law are unmatched—he fights with intelligence, tenacity, and an unshakable commitment to getting the best possible outcome for his clients.

What genuinely distinguishes Brandon is his authentic care for the people he represents. He treated me not simply as a case, but as a person going through one of the most stressful times in my life. He listened carefully, clarified every step clearly, and always put my interests first. His ethics and integrity are beyond reproach; in an area of law in which trust is everything, Brandon is someone you can completely trust.

Despite his outstanding skill and reputation, Brandon remains remarkably reasonable with his billing. He is transparent about fees, works efficiently, and never pads hours or takes advantage of clients in vulnerable positions. He holds firm against aggressive prosecutors and never lets his clients get bullied or pushed around—he protects your rights with strength and professionalism.

Thanks to Brandon's outstanding representation, my situation was resolved far better than it could have been from less attentive, caring, attorneys. I am deeply grateful for his devotion, expertise, and the peace of mind he gave me during a difficult chapter. If you're facing serious legal problems in Wyoming, do yourself a favor and call Brandon Vilos. He is the real deal, and I will forever appreciate everything he did for me.

Rebecca waived a fee for me for what was thankfully brief but invaluable legal advice. She was kind, empathetic, and honest. Her office manager was easy to work with for scheduling.

My experience with attorney Rebecca Swandal has been extremely troubling. Throughout several years of court involvement, she repeatedly misrepresented facts, relied on hearsay, and submitted filings unsupported by admissible evidence.

Even more concerning, Ms. Swandal appeared as a witness in a case in which she was also acting as counsel, a clear violation of the Montana Rules of Professional Conduct, which prohibit an attorney from serving as both advocate and witness in the same matter.

Her conduct showed a disregard for professional ethics, accuracy, and fairness, contributing to unnecessary conflict, confusion, and emotional harm.

While every client deserves strong advocacy, the justice system depends on truth and integrity. Based on my experience, I would strongly recommend finding counsel who upholds ethical boundaries, honesty, and respect for the rule of law

Having made a very big mistake, I started the painful process of trying to find someone who would help without taking my money (happily) and leaving me to my (richly deserved) rewards.  As the saying goes, "play stupid games, win stupid prizes".  Nick took my case, never left me feeling judged by him, and did his best to help me through the process with my dignity and my finances intact.  Nobody could have punished me as passionately as I did and continue to punish myself, and I know I won't be repeating my act of stupidity again.  Nick took my case and obtained for me exactly what I was hoping for...a healthy slap from the justice system that didn't leave me destitute and/or in a cage.  If you find yourself in my shoes, please consider this as a help in sorting through a long list of nameless and faceless counselors.  You'll get his best because that's what his own personal ethics demand of him.  Lawyers get a bad wrap.  I thankfully don't have a lot of experience with them, but if more of them were like Nick, they wouldn't have this kind of reputation.  Please give him a try.  Like me, you'll be glad you did.  Thanks, Nick! And see...I remembered to write this review!  I'll be dropping by to see you one of these days when I'm not desperate for help.  You're a good fellow.  You may not get rich doing good work for dopes like me, but I trust you'll sleep every night knowing you're leaving the world better for your having been a part of it.

To say the least i had no idea of what to do and was an emotional mess.  Through it all Rick stood by me,  offered advise, and was patient.

For example, right up front he advised me to get statement's from her and  her Physician about her mental state as she made here wishes know.  Also was instrumental in getting her home in a survivor-ship deed that made sure the home was in my name at the time of her death.  (I know the terms I used are in correct but you get the idea)

The bottom-line, Rick and your firm provided expert advise and superior customer service.  In the future if I need legal help the first place I plan to go is Jardine, Stephenson, Blewett and Weaver.

reviews.text:
I can't thank Anthony Jackson and AFJ Law Firm enough for representing me in my product liability case against Freedom Arms after I was seriously injured by a defective firearm outside of Kalispell, Montana. The injuries I suffered were devastating and have changed every aspect of my life.

I’m a gun person. I never expected to need a personal injury attorney or to be injured by a defective product. As a lifelong gun owner, I would never have suspected that a modern gun company would be designing its products in a way that its guns can fire without the trigger being pulled and caused to fire by something as simple as hitting a bump in the road while riding in a side-by-side. I also would never have guessed that the company could be sued multiple times, be told by judge and jury that its gun is defective, and still keep making and selling the gun. And I never guessed that a company like this would treat its own customers the way we were treated after I was shot by one of their guns after an inadvertent discharge. This was an incredibly complex case with no insurance coverage, and I now know most personal injury attorneys wouldn't have taken it on or would have dropped it once they found out there was no insurance.

Anthony and his team, especially Kaela, stuck with me for over two years and fought hard every step of the way. They kept costs incredibly low for a products liability case while still putting together expert testimony and depositions that really hurt the defendant's case. What impressed me most was Anthony's commitment to making sure this manufacturer couldn't hide behind non-disclosure agreements anymore. Anthony made sure the dangerous information he uncovered about this defective gun got into the public record so future victims won't have to start from scratch like we did. Anthony also adamantly encouraged me to reject non-disclosure agreements that the company asked for, which have helped the company conceal the facts of what happened. He fought not only for me but also for transparency and the truth. He explained that he wasn’t against guns and normally doesn’t do gun cases, but that he would stand up against a company like this that makes a product that keeps hurting and killing customers who use it in a normal manner that they think is appropriate.

Anthony fought and ensured that Freedom Arms paid a price beyond the settlement. He also established that Freedom Arms can now be sued in other states, not just Wyoming, and their shady practices are now public record. If you need a personal injury lawyer in Bozeman or anywhere in Montana who will fight for you even when the odds are stacked against you, call AFJ Law Firm. Anthony Jackson genuinely cares about his clients and making the world safer, not just getting paid. Thankyou again for all of your hard work greatly appreciated!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, J.C. What happened to you should have never happened - just like it should have never happened to the numerous other people we found out were shot and killed or injured by inadvertent discharges of the Freedom Arms Model 83. We were happy to fight for you against a company that seems to care nothing about the safety and lives of the very customers who they profit from and who refuses to change despite that people keep getting shot when using this gun in a way they thought was safe and which could be done with other single-action revolvers having safety mechanisms implemented over fifty years ago. This company seems like a real outlier in the industry, and it should recall this product, as Ruger did 50 years ago, before more people get hurt or killed.

reviews.text:
I would have given 10 stars if I could!

I was being investigated for a crime that I did not commit and I called several law firms in town before speaking with Abby. The responses(from the receptionist) from the other firms I called was generally "okay, well I'll let ___ attorney know you called and they may get back to you in a week or so" . I understand that people are busy and can't always just jump on a call and having hired multiple lawyers previously (divorce), I was aware that the initial consult(s) can take a while.

I did a small amount of research, and Abby's resume was impressive so I put her on my short list of attorney's to call. When I called her firm, Ryan Miller Law, the receptionist picked up and I thought  "okay, I let her know you called and she'll get back to you". Instead, the receptionist checked with Abby and she immediately took my call and spoke to me.

Again, as mentioned, I've hired several attorney's previous to Abby, so I've gone through the consult process, and they're generally (in my experience) not overly reassuring, but when I spoke with Abby, I could feel my cortisol levels begin to drop. After hearing what was going on, she was confident that she could help, and she did a good job at explaining the process and potential outcomes in layman's terms that I could understand.

I decided to hire her, but doing so was going to be financially difficult as I was already paying two other attorney's (divorce in two states), Abby had sympathy for this and worked with me to set up installments for payment.

Leading up to my interview with the detective, Abby and her wonderful Paralegal, Maya, put everything together with impeccable attention to detail and organization. Abby brought me in to the office to prep for the interview, her and Maya also did an amazing job at not only preparing me, but also helping me feel confident in a very stressful time; the stress relief alone was worth the money!

My interview with the detective went well all due to Abby and Maya's work.

After the interview, Abby continued to provide me with updates and checked in on my general well-being.

Abby will be a force to be reckoned with for a long time, and I couldn't think of anyone else I would want on my side!
reviews.ownerResponse:

reviews.text:
We have been a client of Snyder Beaudry & Cook for the past few years, and I can confidently say that their services have been nothing short of exceptional. Throughout this time, Brant Beaudry has consistently demonstrated professionalism, expertise, and a genuine commitment to his clients.

From the very beginning, Snyder Beaudry & Cook has handled my legal matters with utmost care and diligence. Brant Beaudry, in particular, possesses a deep understanding of the law, and his ability to navigate complex legal issues is truly impressive. Whether it was a routine consultation or a complicated case, Brant always provided clear, concise, and sound legal advice.

What sets Snyder Beaudry & Cook apart is their unwavering dedication to client satisfaction. Brant takes the time to listen to his clients' concerns, thoroughly explains legal options, and tailors his approach to meet individual needs. His responsiveness and accessibility have been particularly reassuring; I always felt that I was in capable hands, no matter the situation.

Moreover, the professionalism exhibited by every member of the firm is commendable. From the attorneys to the support staff, everyone I have interacted with has been courteous, respectful, and efficient. They have gone above and beyond to ensure that my legal matters were resolved in a timely and satisfactory manner.

I would also like to highlight their transparent billing practices. Snyder Beaudry & Cook has always been upfront about costs, providing detailed explanations and ensuring there were no surprises. This level of transparency and integrity is rare and greatly appreciated.

Overall, I highly recommend Snyder Beaudry & Cook to anyone in need of legal services. Their exceptional expertise, client-centered approach, and unwavering professionalism make them a top-notch law firm. I am incredibly grateful for the outstanding service they have provided over the years and look forward to continuing our relationship in the future.

Thank you, Brant Beaudry, and the entire team at Snyder Beaudry & Cook, for your excellent work and dedication.

If I could give zero stars, I would. This law office took my money and did absolutely nothing to advocate for me. I was dealing with a custody case where the opposing attorney worked hard to make me look like a bad mother — and unfortunately, my own attorney did nothing to stop it. I provided them with clear evidence that the opposing party had made multiple false reports to law enforcement and Child & Family Services — reports that amounted to harassment — and they still refused to file anything in my defense.

Instead of helping me, they fired me, claiming I was asking them to do things they “weren’t willing to do.” Apparently, asking them to actually defend me and hold the other party accountable was too much. I had to represent myself in court, and despite all of this, I still secured full custody of my child — on my own. This law firm played no part in that success and absolutely failed to protect me when I needed legal help the most. Save your time, your money, and your sanity — go somewhere else.

Don't question them, or they will really loose all sense of reality, and then gaslight you to think they have answered all your questions, even though they just talk in circles around actually god for bid, DOING ANYTHING!  Attorneys like this firm is exactly what gives attorneys a bad name.  Negligent at best!
Nice
Polite and Professional

Brad and his staff have helped our family through some difficult times since 2018. We couldn't have gotten through all the necessary paperwork and issues without his guidance. I've sent others to him knowing that he's willing to help others just as much.
Overwhelmed with my experience, Brad and team was the pause I needed! Cut and Dried the Law is the Law get the team that knows it! Bulman Jones & Cook

Bulman, Jones & Cook does good work when they do it. They seem to be very busy, but, maybe too busy.

When I first meet with them they sounded friendly, motivated, and proactive. Later I found if I don’t constantly drive them nothing gets done. I’m lost in the shuffle of clients.

Frequently, things are completed last minute and have often been very low quality, though the quality has improved over time.

Communication is low. If I don’t reach out I rarely hear anything. I feel like my case is not a priority to get done.

My attorney often sounds like he is running from task to task all day. He probably is since he has a heavy load. But this also means he does not pay attention to my requests. Years go by and things don’t get done. Requests are forgotten.

If you can get them to do the work it will be good. There are better firms who do higher quality work in the area.

Speaking for my late wife and our children, I can’t properly put into words the dedication your firm has put into our Will and helping us all during difficult times. I feel your firm went above and beyond our expectations to get us through those times. Very professional and compassionate. I have and will continue to recommend your services to others. Thank you so much to all of you from the Kenmir family.

I worked with Spencer for a family law case and he and the rest of the team were so helpful. They made the process as easy as possible. They were very transparent about the process and had excellent communication with me throughout the process. I would definitely use their services again!

Really, Really, Nice Group of Folks.
Good Law Office.
Spencer's  Comportment & Professionalism is Wonderful.

Brad Jones was recommended to me by a friend who had also gone through a complicated international divorce and child custody case. From the first meeting, he was extremely knowledgeable and professional, and I immediately felt at ease handing my case over to him. He was confident, organized, and very assertive in handling both the legal process and the opposing attorney. I always felt well represented and well informed. I would absolutely use Brad again in the future and I have already recommended him to friends.
Efficient friendly staff, plus Atty Bradford was not only a good listener but is a very capable legal mind. I’ll use him again for other legal help, I’m sure.
Spencer and his staff were highly professional handling our case. Would absolutely recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Jimmy,

Thank you so much for your kind words! We’re thrilled to hear that you found our team professional in handling your case. Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we’re here to help should you need any assistance in the future. Best wishes!

Thanks,

reviews.text: Very helpful answered all the questions I had
Asked for help and they said call the suicide hotline. Most useless and immature lawyers ever

Brad is an amazing and extremely talented attorney, there is a reason he has his own law firm. When I was assaulted at work I told him what happened, he said I had a winnable case he would be happy to represent me on. It took some time just like most legal matters do, but he gave me sound advice and advised me on what the outcome would be, which was spot on. I have not hesitated to give others his name for other legal matters.

If zero stars was an option I would give that. Brad was my husband’s attorney for nearly 5 years, as mentioned in another reviews his lack of action, failure to communicate, and not filing motions necessary to keep a case moving. Brad didn’t listen to any concerns voiced, shut down requests, and more than anything never seemed to support the what the end goal was. If it were left up to Brad my husband would be sitting behind bars because he never communicated important information regarding the case. We’ve ultimately retained new counsel. If you’re looking for a family law attorney run, Brad Jones is not your guy.
Would not recommend, lately things have been missed or not taken care of in a timely manner forcing us to seek another attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: Scott, as you were not my client I cannot substantively respond to your concerns due to client confidentiality issues.

reviews.text: In hindsight I am disappointed with the lack of action from Brad Jones. He did not file things in a timely matter and I am getting screwed due to his lack of oversight.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Miles, as you recall I asked you to find a new attorney.  I can only assume that you are referring to untimely discovery responses in your review above.  I requested that you provide my office your answer to discovery by January 27, 2024 in order to review and forward the information to opposing counsel when due on February 7, 2024.  You never responded.  Your significant other ultimately provided the information weeks after the due date and only after opposing counsel filed a motion to compel your responses.  I am sorry that things have not gone well for you.

reviews.text: I called to get some advice for Employee workers rights, and Thomas Bulman steered me in the right direction. Great person would highly recommend seeking advice or legal services.
I went to Bulman Jones & Cook after suffering injuries in an automobile accident. They bargained with the insurance company aggressively, and I got more than I had anticipated.

I give them a 4, because they did not take my case, but I appreciate the advice I received, and the feedback on what to expect. Very knowledgeable, and helpful, and from the brief conversation we had, sounds like an easy person to get along with.

Bieber Law responded to questions we had after moving out of state regarding updating our will. Bill was very helpful and answered our questions even though  we didn't hire him to work for us. Thank you, Bill.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Abby!

He effectively represented me to an acceptable settlement in the case.

Brian was great to work with. I thought my case would be long and drawn out however, start to finish it took 4 weeks and ended in my favor!! If you need a lawyer who knows what needs to be done, hire Brian Miller.

reviews.text: When I met Brian Miller and explained my case to him, he provided me with the professionalism I was seeking. He was very responsive whenever I had a concern. I would not hesitate to recommend Brian, oh and by the way we won!!!

A while back  I had a problem with a big added insurance company about not wanting to pay to have my engine replaced that was on my contract.  I was having trouble getting a lawyer to take my case.  I got in contact with  Brian Miller company and in less than 15 min. Brian Miller called me back and said he will take my case and would win for me.  He told me not to worry any more about it.  Brian Miller didn't just win this case and get my money back for the engine, he got me more.  He really likes to help the little man and makes sure he gets a fair shake.

Brian is an excellent advocate for his clients, with calm thinking and smart insight into intricate issues. He pulls no punches in getting you to the truth and is focused on making a case for you successful.

Russ basically allowed the state to bully me & totally screw me over completely by not pushing to help me get the necessary care I needed after a work related accident. I got run down by several steers working for a rancher. I am now disabled due to not getting the proper care. My back is totally messed up for life! Thanks, Russ, for NOT doing your job & for not standing up for your client!!
reviews.ownerResponse: I reviewed your file regarding my representation of you for a workers’ comp injury dated 07-25-2013. The claim was denied by the Montana State Fund. According to the publicly available settlement documents approved by the department of labor and industry, a medical examination by an orthopedic surgeon and a neuropsychological evaluation by a neuropsychologist were obtained. Both of these evaluations concluded your medical complaints were not related to the work injury. Following receipt of these records you and I settled your claim with the State Fund in May of 2014 for $25,000 on a disputed liability basis. I’m sorry if you now, 11 years later, feel like that was an inadequate settlement but I diligently advocated for you, as I do for all of my clients.

reviews.text: Extremely disappointed with this firm. Mindi (Melissa) "represented" me in my case against a former employer. My HRB claim was almost closed without my knowledge because she failed to meet multiple deadlines with the state. She has refused to communicate, and I'd be lucky to get a response after multiple emails and texts. I was forced to call the firm and leave her messages. It's of concern that she's not listed on the website. It should also be noted that due to her lack of professionalism and effort I am now looking for another lawyer. I must explain to new counsel that her incompetence led to an improper investigation and failure to provide the HRB process with full evidence of my claims. Please don't risk a Pro Bono situation with her.
Friendly understanding and helpful.
Best Attorney in his field
Russ Plath Law answered my questions quickly when other law firms dragged their feet.  Thank you!  Highly recommended!

Everyone here is very professional, friendly and hard working but Mindi Driscoll has went ABOVE AND BEYOND to help me with my case regarding custody with my daughter! Not only did she take this case on pro-Bono, she was an absolute dream to work with. She is very compassionate, creative, super responsive and  very strong willed to bring light to an extreme dark tunnel! She brought much happiness and relief to my family with the accomplishments she received for us. I couldn’t of asked for anyone better to have defend me! I wish I could give more than 5 stars!!

I think Russ might have been a help to me but I had ms. Driscoll and she did nothing but argue with me and give excuses why she wasn't doing anything, at one point called me a bully because I asked about a letter she was going to write 3 weeks earlier, I  tried to call and talk to russ about the situation and she intercepted the call and informed me I wouldn't be talking to her boss,I  would warn to steer clear of this girl.

This man said my case was cut and dry! Told me to tell my previous attorney he was representing me so he could get the evidence sent to him. Then took off mtn climbing for 2 months, then came back, never got in touch with me, I ccontacted him and he asked for more evidence, which he got,wasted 2 more months and then said he doesn't have time to litigate my case. No reference, nothing. Wasted all my time in a time sentsitive matter and quit!! Less than 0 Stars
I'm in the process of using him for another case. So needless to say I thinks hes pretty darn good at what he does

We began working with Shahid at Border Crossing Law in 2021 to file the paperwork for my daughter’s green card during the height of COVID, and the process turned out to be very long and complicated. Throughout the entire journey, Shahid was knowledgeable, patient, and incredibly responsive. He always took the time to speak with us, promptly answered our emails, and met with us in several video conferences where he clearly explained our options and the decisions we needed to make.

Our case was extremely complex, and near the end, we needed a vaccination exemption, which he successfully helped us obtain. Thanks to his expertise and guidance, everything was finally approved.

If you are looking for an immigration attorney who truly knows what he is doing and will guide you every step of the way, I highly recommend Shahid and Border Crossing Law.

I'm not one to give businesses a bad review, but with all this immigration chaos, I want to make sure the public knows which attorneys are good and which ones don't take cases seriously. I hired Shahid Hoque for filing a few applications for me, like VAWA, U-visa, and DACA. At first, it was pretty normal not to hear much from attorney Shahid Hoque, but then the new administration decided to reopen cases, and mine was one of them, with only a couple of months' notice. One thing about me is that I like to get things done quickly. Mr. Shahid never picked up the phone once and wouldn't even communicate like an attorney should, which was very frustrating when you're depending on an attorney to help with your upcoming court case. My previous attorney was still on file, so he was the one to alert me about my cases being reopened. The point of my story is that I retained Mr. Shahid Hoque to represent me in court, and all his motions were denied. So, my previous attorney took over the case and was there everyday.  Mr Shahid was horrible at communicating with me and waited to the last minute to respond regarding my case the following day. When I asked about a refund for my representation "wich he did not represent me" he said there was no refund. All in all I will never recommend this law firm hire an attorney that doesn't just take your money but is attentive and answers the phone when you call and its really thete for you when you really need them. Very dissapointed to the way I was treated by this guy. No stars ✨️
reviews.ownerResponse: You cannot please everyone all the time, but this is a gross mischaracterization of the representation. As you note, you did not initially hire me for removal proceedings, and it is difficult when clients are not forthcoming about their past removal proceedings, which can be reopened.  Any rate, we do wish you the best of luck.

reviews.text:
Shahid helped me with my case and guided me through every step of the filing process. He was very prompt in responding to my questions and made sure I understood exactly what to  expect throughout the process.

I highly recommend their services.

The entire time felt like I was corresponding with a higher being. Perfect spelling, grammar, punctuality, mannerisms, and delivery of service. I could not be happier if I tried. Thank you for protecting our family when we needed it.

Shahid was very helpful and thoughtful-he made the immigration process much easier to handle. He was also very kind and understanding of the various stresses that emerge during the waiting process. Very grateful we decided to use their services-as opposed to doing it on our own!

Shahid and Border Crossing Law Firm went above and beyond for our family to secure green cards and citizenship for our children and I could not be more pleased with the outcome and their attention to detail.
Quick responses, knowledge of immigration law and efficiency are why I would definitely recommend them.

I very highly recommend working with him to guide you through the immigration process.

**⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5 Stars)**

Thank you so much Shahid, my family is extremely grateful! He was so helpful during both the process of green cards for my mom and my dad. He is great and I highly recommend.

I'm forever grateful to have had Mr. Shahid represent me in my asylum case. From the initial consultation to the final approval, Mr. Shahid demonstrated exceptional expertise, compassion, and dedication.

His guidance and support throughout the process was invaluable, and his ability to explain complex legal concepts in a clear and concise manner helped alleviate my anxiety.

What impressed me most was his genuine interest in my well-being and his commitment to achieving the best possible outcome. His  professionalism, responsiveness, and attention to detail are truly exceptional.

I highly recommend Mr Shahid to anyone seeking a knowledgeable, empathetic, and results-driven immigration attorney. Thank you again for helping me achieve my dream!
Shahid saved our family from years of uncertainty and gave new light to our future!
Great firm to work with , very efficient !!

Unprofessional and a waste of money. The immigration process would have been smoother and nicer if we had done it ourselves. I highly recommend that anyone who is in need of an immigration lawyer to rethink and do it yourself or contact Chris at Immigration Law of Montana in Shepherd. We wanted to continue working with him but were financially unable. This was the only other option but it would have been better if we had done it ourselves. I urge whoever is looking into this service to go elsewhere. Shahid is unprofessional, bad at communication, and doesn’t respond promptly or well. Everything he does is through an automated system so he isn’t doing any work for his fee anyway. Save your money and patience and do it yourself.
